content.summaryText: Community Vector Control in Vector-Borne Diseases focuses on early recognition of endemic vector-borne illness patterns. Primary objective: Identify breeding hotspots.

content.questionText: In Community Vector Control, which action best supports 'Identify breeding hotspots'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Screen travel/exposure history and warning signs
Ignoring local epidemiology during early triage
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: screen travel/exposure history and warning signs. Practical emphasis: Promote household prevention.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when bleeding signs, neurologic symptoms, organ dysfunction, or severe fever.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
